creator: root@cooltrainer.org
text:
Samba 3.4 (and 3.3 and possibly others) binaries dump core when executed. The culprit seems to be compiling with -pie on ARM.

Samba also does not seem to respect my configure argument disabling pie from the port's makefile.


Example:
pochan# smbpasswd 
Illegal instruction (core dumped)

Fix: 

Since the configure argument to disable pie was not honored I had to remove -pie from the Makefile manually after configure.

There were five occurrences:
[root@Emi#samba34]grep pie work/samba-3.4.8/source3/**/Makefile
work/samba-3.4.8/source3/Makefile:LDFLAGS=-pie -Wl,-z,relro -L/usr/local/lib -Wl,-R/usr/local/lib -L/usr/local/lib -L./bin -L/usr/local/lib
work/samba-3.4.8/source3/Makefile:	@$(CC) -pie -o $@ $(REPLACETORT_OBJ) $(LDFLAGS) \
work/samba-3.4.8/source3/Makefile:	@$(CC) -pie -o $@ $(SMBCONFTORT_OBJ) $(LDFLAGS) \
work/samba-3.4.8/source3/lib/netapi/examples/Makefile:LDFLAGS=-pie -L/usr/local/lib -Wl,-R/usr/local/lib -L/usr/local/lib -L./bin -L/usr/local/lib
work/samba-3.4.8/source3/lib/netapi/tests/Makefile:LDFLAGS=-pie -L/usr/local/lib -Wl,-R/usr/local/lib -L/usr/local/lib -L./bin -L/usr/local/lib
How-To-Repeat: Install Samba through Ports
